content: Swift zsh -lc quoting for Process: Process.arguments = ["-lc", commandString]. The shell tokenizes commandString itself, so the arguments array has exactly two elements: the flag string and the entire command. Paths with spaces go into the command string substituted as single-quoted: let quoted = "'" + absolutePath.replacingOccurrences(of: "'", with: "'\\''" ) + "'". This is POSIX single-quote escaping safe for any path. For {wav} substitution: replace the literal {wav} token in the user command string with the quoted path before passing to -lc. Login shell (-l) reads ~/.zshrc etc, so Homebrew-installed tools on PATH work even from a GUI app.
